businessSummary.oneLiner: Southeast Asia's largest e-commerce (Shopee), digital entertainment (Garena/Free Fire), and fintech (Monee) operator, a Singapore-based global internet company
businessSummary.description: Sea Limited is a Singapore-headquartered global technology company. Q4 2025 results released 3/3: revenue $6.85B (+38.4% YoY), EPS $0.63. Revenue beat but EPS missed consensus by $0.28, triggering a sharp sell-off on 3/3 followed by a modest rebound. FY2025 revenue $22.94B (+36.4%), net income $1.58B (+255%). Monee loan book $9.2B (+80%), 90-day NPL 1.1%. BofA cut target from $200 to $182 while maintaining Buy. P/E 35.02x, Forward P/E 20.79x, PEG 0.35.
howTheyMakeMoney: E-commerce (Shopee): No.1 e-commerce platform in Southeast Asia. GMV-based commissions and advertising. Q4 revenue +35.8%
Digital Financial Services (Monee/SeaMoney): Digital payments, lending, insurance. Q4 revenue +54.3%, loan book $9.2B (+80%)
Digital Entertainment (Garena): Free Fire and other game operations. Q4 bookings +23.8% YoY
Others: Logistics (Shopee Express), food delivery, etc.
keyProducts: Shopee: No.1 e-commerce platform in Southeast Asia and Latin America
Free Fire: Globally popular battle royale mobile game
SeaMoney/Monee: Digital payment and financial services, loan book $9.2B

keyMetrics.name: P/E Ratio (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Reasonable for a high-growth stock even after rebound

keyMetrics.name: PEG Ratio
keyMetrics.interpretation: Well below 1, significantly undervalued relative to growth

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Improving profitability but VIP program investment pressuring margins

keyMetrics.name: FCF
keyMetrics.interpretation: Strong cash generation. Ample reinvestment capacity

growthDrivers.title: Q4 2025 Revenue $6.85B (+38.4%) Beat, but EPS Miss
growthDrivers.description: Revenue beat consensus by $420M, but EPS of $0.63 missed expectations by $0.28 due to VIP program investment and increased Monee provisions.

growthDrivers.title: Monee AI-Powered Lending Acceleration, Loan Book $9.2B (+80%)
growthDrivers.description: AI-assisted underwriting expanding borrower base. 37M active credit users, 5.8M new borrowers in Q4. NPL 1.1% remains healthy.

growthDrivers.title: FY2025 Net Income +255% Turnaround
growthDrivers.description: Annual net income reached $1.58B, a 3.5x increase YoY. Structural profitability improvement confirmed.

growthDrivers.title: Strong Buy Upgrade with YE26 Target $136
growthDrivers.description: StockAnalysis upgraded to Strong Buy post-selloff on undervaluation. 54% upside from current levels.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: Southeast Asia's No.1 e-commerce player (Shopee), benefiting from 670M population digitalization. E-commerce-fintech-gaming ecosystem synergy is the core moat.

moats.description: Shopee's two-sided seller-buyer network. More sellers attract more buyers and vice versa.
Shopee (e-commerce) feeds SeaMoney (payments/lending) feeds ShopeeFood (delivery), creating an integrated ecosystem.
No.1 e-commerce position across 6 Southeast Asian markets. Self-built logistics infrastructure via Shopee Express.

competitors.name: TikTok Shop/Lazada
competitors.detail: Rapidly growing social commerce in Southeast Asia. Shopee's biggest threat

competitors.name: MercadoLibre (MELI)
competitors.detail: Latin America's No.1 e-commerce platform. Direct competitor in Brazil

competitors.name: Grab (GRAB)
competitors.detail: Southeast Asia super-app. Competes in fintech and delivery

competitors.name: Alibaba/Lazada
competitors.detail: Alibaba's capital backing aggressive Southeast Asia push

recentNews.headline: SE shares rebound +5.1% as investors reassess post-earnings selloff
recentNews.significance: Market views selloff as overdone, refocusing on +38% revenue growth and profitability improvements

recentNews.headline: 2026 outlook: Shopee GMV +25% target, EBITDA at least matching 2025 levels
recentNews.significance: Balance between logistics/membership/fintech investment and profitability is key debate

recentNews.headline: Jefferies cuts price target to $150 on 2026 outlook concerns
SE plunges 26.8% after Q4 earnings, biggest drop since 2023
recentNews.significance: EPS miss and softer 2026 Shopee outlook triggered massive sell-off

recentNews.headline: BofA cuts target from $200 to $182, maintains Buy rating
recentNews.significance: Target lowered but Buy maintained, still implying significant upside

analystOpinions.notableComment: 30 analysts average target of $189.84 implies +106% upside. Morgan Stanley maintains Overweight at $173, Jefferies cuts to $150, BofA holds at $182. Post-earnings target revisions ongoing.

risks.title: Continued selling pressure after EPS miss
risks.description: Q4 EPS of $0.63 missed consensus by $0.28. Despite rebound, short-term volatility remains elevated.

risks.title: 2026 Shopee growth deceleration concerns
risks.description: GMV +25% guidance decelerates from +38% in 2025. Margin vs. growth balance is critical.

risks.title: Monee credit risk
risks.description: Loan book $9.2B (+80%) rapid growth with provisions up +66.7%. Potential for rising NPLs in economic downturn.

risks.title: Intensifying Southeast Asian e-commerce competition
risks.description: TikTok Shop and Lazada/Alibaba aggressively expanding. Bloomberg cites competition as key driver of EPS miss.

risks.title: Share dilution
risks.description: Share count increased +5.54% YoY. Ongoing stock-based compensation costs.

buyReasons.title: PEG 0.35, extreme undervaluation
buyReasons.rationale: 38%+ growth at P/E 36x. Among the most undervalued high-growth stocks globally.

buyReasons.title: Analyst target $190, +106% upside
buyReasons.rationale: 25 of 30 analysts rate Buy. Market consensus is that the selloff was overdone.

buyReasons.title: FY2025 net income +255% turnaround
buyReasons.rationale: Structural profitability improvement confirmed. Q4 miss may prove temporary.

buyReasons.title: Technical rebound initiated after earnings crash
buyReasons.rationale: +5.1% bounce from oversold territory signals investor reassessment. Potential bottom formation.

overallOpinion: SE rebounded +5.1% to $92.30 after a 26.8% crash on 3/3, as investors shift focus from EPS miss to +38% revenue growth.
PEG of 0.35 and analyst consensus target of $190 (+106% upside) signal extreme undervaluation. 25 of 30 analysts maintain Buy.
2026 Shopee GMV guidance of +25% and intensifying TikTok Shop competition are key variables. Jefferies cut its target to $150.
Technically, trading well below both the 50-day SMA ($119) and 200-day SMA ($147). RSI at 35.8 suggests early-stage recovery from oversold conditions.

sources.description: SE technical analysis, RSI, moving averages

sources.description: SE real-time quotes and financial data

x.hook: SE $92, post-crash rebound begins. Oversold recovery?
x.text: $SE (3/6) Rebounded +5.1% to $92.30 after a brutal 26.8% crash. Revenue grew +38% but one EPS miss sent it near 52-week lows. Did the market overreact?
thread: Valuation speaks: PEG 0.35 means you're paying 1/3 of its growth rate. 25 of 30 analysts say Buy with an average target of $190, that's +106% upside from here.
The 2026 question: Shopee GMV growth slowing from +38% to +25%. TikTok Shop competition intensifying. Jefferies cut their target to $150.
Technicals: Trading well below the 50-day moving average ($119) and 200-day ($147). RSI at 35.8, early stages of climbing out of oversold territory. Whether this bounce holds is the key question.

fact.hook: SE $92, post-crash rebound begins. Oversold recovery?
fact.text: $SE (3/6) Rebounded +5.1% to $92.30 after a brutal 26.8% crash. Revenue grew +38% but one EPS miss sent it near 52-week lows. Did the market overreact?
thread: Valuation speaks: PEG 0.35 means you're paying 1/3 of its growth rate. 25 of 30 analysts say Buy with an average target of $190, that's +106% upside from here.
The 2026 question: Shopee GMV growth slowing from +38% to +25%. TikTok Shop competition intensifying. Jefferies cut their target to $150.
Technicals: Trading well below the 50-day moving average ($119) and 200-day ($147). RSI at 35.8, early stages of climbing out of oversold territory. Whether this bounce holds is the key question.

witty.hook: SE grew revenue 38% and the market rewarded it with a 27% haircut
witty.text: $SE (3/6) Grew revenue 38%, and the market said 'cool, here's a 26.8% drop.' Then the next day it bounced +5.1%. The market is basically an emotional trader who panic-sells and then regrets it.
thread: PEG of 0.35. You're literally paying a third of the growth rate. 25 analysts screaming Buy at $190 while the stock sits at $92. Someone's wrong, and it's probably not all 25 of them.
The bear case: Shopee growth slowing from 38% to 25%, TikTok Shop eating market share. Jefferies says $150. Fair points, but 25% GMV growth is still... growth.
Below 50-day ($119) and 200-day ($147) moving averages with RSI climbing from oversold. Is this a real bottom or just a dead cat bounce? Next week tells the story.

empathy.hook: SE holders, that crash was brutal. But the rebound gives a glimmer of hope
empathy.text: $SE (3/6) Down 26.8% in a single day. If you held through that, it was one of the toughest weeks in a while. Today's +5.1% rebound is a small comfort, but there's still a long road ahead.
thread: Revenue up 38% and you get punished like this? It feels unfair. But that's how earnings work. The market trades expectations, not results.
The good news: PEG 0.35, 25 analysts say Buy, target $190. The hard truth: sitting well below the 50-day ($119) and 200-day ($147) moving averages.
2026 Shopee growth slowing to +25%. Not bad in absolute terms, but lower than what the market had priced in. Managing expectations is half the game.
The rebound has started. The question is whether to add here or wait for confirmation. There's no shame in waiting for the trend to establish itself.
Long-term, this is Southeast Asia's No.1 e-commerce player with exploding fintech growth. The fundamentals haven't changed. Time and patience may be your best allies here.
